# OpenTelemetry mysqlclient Instrumentation

{{ pypi }}

## Installation

```
pip install opentelemetry-instrumentation-mysqlclient
```

modules.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysqlclient
documentation:
The integration with MySQLClient supports the [MySQLClient] library and can be enabled
by using `MySQLClientInstrumentor`.

# Usage

```python
import MySQLdb
from opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysqlclient import MySQLClientInstrumentor


MySQLClientInstrumentor().instrument()

cnx = MySQLdb.connect(database="MySQL_Database")
cursor = cnx.cursor()
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO test (testField) VALUES (123)"
cnx.commit()
cursor.close()
cnx.close()
```

# API

[mysqlclient]: https://pypi.org/project/MySQLClient/

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysqlclient.MySQLClientInstrumentor._instrument
documentation:
Integrate with the mysqlclient library.
https://github.com/PyMySQL/mysqlclient/

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysqlclient.MySQLClientInstrumentor._uninstrument
documentation: "Disable mysqlclient instrumentation

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysqlclient.MySQLClientInstrumentor.instrument_connection
documentation:
Enable instrumentation in a mysqlclient connection.

Args:
    connection: The connection to instrument.
    tracer_provider: The optional tracer provider to use. If omitted
        the current globally configured one is used.

Returns:
    An instrumented connection.

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysqlclient.MySQLClientInstrumentor.uninstrument_connection
documentation:
Disable instrumentation in a mysqlclient connection.

Args:
    connection: The connection to uninstrument.

Returns:
    An uninstrumented connection.
